description:
Parent epic fz-rh2.21 has the full design; eli5.html at the repo root is the design doc; requires fz-rh2.21.1 (waiting extends) and fz-rh2.21.2 (rebase) landed. This ticket installs the old-world lesson ABSENCE NEVER BECOMES A TYPE and "ANY IS EARNED" in the body walk of AnalyzeActivation (src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs) — as deletions and one honest Option, not as imported machinery. Background: the previous-generation engine (src/type_infer/mod.rs, module-doc section "Unknown is not none") kept a four-state cell {Pending, Unknown, NoReturn, Known}; compiler2 collapsed all of it onto bare Ty with three lies: absent-callee-return read as the TYPE none (a false "no value can exist" fact that drops calls as dead), any() handed back as a placeholder on five wait paths, and valueless paths sharing the empty type's representation. We adopt ONE no-evidence state where the old world had three, because compiler2's fact settledness carries completeness per-fact (seal_semantic_closure already refuses unsettled facts) — the old-world Info enum, absorbing Unknown, and global status scan are explicitly NOT to be imported.

THE SHAPE. Path results become Option-typed; value cells stay as they are (SemanticValue today; collapsed later by fz-rh2.21.8). Specifically:
- analyze_entry, analyze_tail, deliver_tail_value return Option<Ty> (None = "no evidence on this path yet": pending callee, halt, dead arm — all join-identity). A None result short-circuits delivery exactly where the is_empty(flow) check at the top of deliver_tail_value short-circuits today — which means call ARGUMENTS are always real types (a path that would pass a no-evidence value never reaches the call).
- Term::Halt-equivalent (LoweredTail::Halt) returns None (today: none type).
- The clause-fold in analyze_activation (today: let mut return_ty = none_ty(world); ... if is_empty(return_ty) { clause_return } else { union(...) }) becomes an Option fold: None ⊔ x = x; Some(a) ⊔ Some(b) = Some(types.union(a, b)). The same dance appears in merge_observed_ty, the LoweredTail::If/Dispatch/Receive joins, and resolve_closure_call's return accumulation — convert all of them; the is_empty bottom-guards MELT (this is the point: they existed because the empty type doubled as the fold identity).
- The final activation return is Option<Ty>; ALWAYS claim the ReturnType fact in outputs (ownership is constant); the STORE value is Option<Ty> (None = no evidence yet). KLEENE NOTE for the doc-comment: bottom and the empty type coincide AT the fixpoint — a settled None-valued return legitimately means "provably never returns" (e.g. fn f(), do: f()) and downstream may materialize it as none; they differ only mid-climb, where readers treat None as the join identity and are re-woken if it rises. The temporal marker is settledness, not the value.

THE CALL READ. prepare_function_call currently does reads.push(ReturnType(key)); world.activation_return(&key).unwrap_or_else(none). New: return Option<Ty> = the store value (None when the fact is absent OR the store holds no evidence); KEEP the read registration (the subscription is what re-wakes the caller when the callee's return rises — chaotic iteration needs no waits on returns and therefore cannot deadlock on mutual recursion; do NOT convert this to a wait). The callsite summary is STILL emitted when the return is None — demand must flow so the callee gets analyzed — therefore CallSiteSummary.return_ty becomes Option<Ty> (src/compiler2/semantic.rs struct CallSiteSummary and CallTargetSummary): an honest snapshot that may contain unresolved edges. Consumers: seal_semantic_closure walks summaries only when settled; materialization (jobs/artifact.rs) reads summaries post-seal and may expect Some with an expect() documenting the settledness invariant — find every consumer via the compiler and give each the honest treatment (no unwrap_or(any)).

THE DEAD-CODE CHECKS BECOME TRUE: resolve_direct_call's empty-arg drop (if any arg is_empty -> no emission, dead path) and deliver_tail_value's is_empty short-circuit KEEP their text but now fire only on the PROVEN empty type (a contract contradiction, a proven-dead arm), because absence can no longer manufacture none. Do not delete them; document them as proofs.

ANY IS EARNED — deletions:
- resolve_function_call and resolve_protocol_call have arms returning Ok((None, Vec::new(), any_ty(world))) on missing-state paths (unresolved module, missing contract, missing protocol dispatch, missing impl owner). Each becomes: register the wait (as today) and contribute a None path result. The any() placeholders are deleted. (They existed because waits used to destroy the job's prior work, so the code limped forward to publish something; fz-rh2.21.1 removed that pressure.)
- resolve_closure_call: unresolvable callable (callable_value_clauses None) currently returns any. This one is a genuine boundary judgment: KEEP any here for now (a dynamic callable value is closer to an earned dynamic edge than to pending evidence) but leave a doc-comment marking it as the remaining earned-any site alongside provider boundaries (refine_call_return / provider-boundary arms keep contract_return.unwrap_or(any) — that IS the earned public edge).
- value_fact's unwrap_or(any) default for missing env entries: a ValueId missing from scope means its defining path produced no evidence this round — the entry cannot be analyzed; make entry_scope construction in analyze_entry detect a required-but-absent capture and return None for the whole entry (path-no-evidence), then make value_fact's default unreachable (expect with a message stating the invariant).
- The three staged early-bail blocks in analyze_activation (if !waits.is_empty() { return partial wait-effects } after the clause walk, after coalesce_call_emissions, after escaping-callable resolution) MELT: with extends-semantics and None-paths, waits simply accumulate into the final JobEffects and partial evidence publishes safely; let the function fall through to its single conclusion. Verify coalesce_call_emissions and the emission loop behave with waits pending (they only consume computed summaries; they do).

KNOCK-ON SITES (find by compiler, fix honestly, no adapters): merge_observed_ty (becomes the Option join or is deleted into it), refine_call_return (Option-aware: None observed stays None; contract refinement applies only to Some), rebuild_coalesced_call_emission / merge_call_targets (target return_ty is Option — merge via Option join), executable_callsite_needs and seal_semantic_closure reading summary returns, activation telemetry rendering (return state is now Option — emit the raw Option via opaque, never format it at the emit site).

TESTS (TDD):
- A drive_test where a recursive function's first analysis round reads its own absent return: assert via captured callsite.defined telemetry that the self-callsite summary carries return None (not the none TYPE), and that no call is dropped (the callee demand fact appears).
- A test pinning fn f(), do: f() (or IR-level equivalent): drives to Resolved; settled ReturnType store is None; downstream treats it as never-returns without diagnostics.
- A test that a missing contract mid-walk produces a wait and PARTIAL published evidence (clause results from other paths) rather than an any-typed return — assert no any appears in the published summary returns (capture handler downcasts the summaries).
- Existing canary compiler2_quicksort_root_closes_with_a_finite_recursive_frontier (drive_test) must stay green: exactly 2 append activations in the closure.
Acceptance: green per epic baseline, zero warnings, one commit. Expected interplay: with fz-rh2.21.4 not yet landed, returns are still last-write; the oscillation may still be reachable — the convergence assertion lands in fz-rh2.21.5.
title: semantic analysis: honest paths — absence never becomes a type
